content_body:
Downtown Market Blue Ridge

(June - November)

The Blue Ridge Farmer’s Market is a community market for farmers, hobby gardeners, artists and crafters. The market is open from 8:00am to 12:00pm on Saturdays from June through November and is located in the downtown City Park in front of the courthouse.

This is a great time to support and shop local - be it produce or other items for the home. The true meaning of from farm to table, and in the backyard of many locals, taking pride in their ability to cultivate and share the fruit of their labor and resources. 

 

Georgia Mountain Fair

(August 17th - August 27th)

The Georgia Mountain Fairgrounds proudly hosts first class musical performances, arts & crafts, fun carnival rides, unique attractions and a glimpse into North Georgia’s rich history and culture. Scoop up your friends and pac up the family for a fun and memorable North Georgia Experience!

Events also include the 26th Annual Blue Ridge Rodeo on August 19th and 20th, brought to you by the Kiwanis Club of BLue Ridge & Mountain Valley Motors. Proudly roping since 1995!  Professional rodeo cowboys and cowgirls will be competing in a variety of events including bronc riding, calf roping, team roping, barrel racing, bull riding, and much more!

 

Georgia Mountain Classics Car Cruise-In ~ DQ

(August 26th)

The 4th Friday of every month, March through September, stop by and check out restored, classic cars from T-birds to muscle, trucks to racecares, with the Georgia Mountain Classics Car Cruise. Great fun for the whole family. Free event, but at least buy one of DQ’s dessert - you can never go wrong with a soft serve blizzard! Free registration if you want to bring your classic car and you get a $4.00 coupon for DQ! 

A group of classic car enthusiasts from the Blue Ridge Georgia Mountains. Dinner meetings are the 3rd Tuesday of each month in Blue Ridge. CruiseIns are the 4th Friday between March and September at the Dairy Queen in Blue Ridge. Car Show is downtown Blue Ridge on the 2nd Saturday in MA. And the Cruise For Kids is the first Saturday in December.

 

Ray Laban Live! At Cucinella’s Pizzeria

(August 27th)

Come out and join us for some great good and live music with guitarist Ray Lebean! Singer/songwriter playing classics from the 70s. Songs by James Taylor, Jim Croce, Gorgon Lightfoot, and more, with over 55 years of experience! 

Cucinella’s Pizzeria is a family tradition since 1932. Family atmosphere located in the new Trackside Station beside the downtown Blue Ridge City Park. Hand tossed, brick oven pizza’s with a wide variety of toppings along with they specialty pizza’s: Calzone, Margherita, Veggie LOvers, Spinach, & more! They even offer a 10” Cauliflower Gluten Free crust with your choice of toppings!
title: Things to Do in Blue Ridge GA in August 2022
